era: 1990-1997. Grunge/Punk. E=7 from velocity. Seattle. Empty Records (Fred Canby, founded 1988). Dave Dederer (guitar, pre-PUSA). Underground punk end of the grunge spectrum. Fast tempos, raw production, short songs. Dissolved 1997.

theory_nodes.concept: EMPTY RECORDS CONTEXT. Empty Records (founded 1988, Fred Canby) operated outside Sub Pop's commercial infrastructure and the major-label system. Smaller budgets, faster recordings, rawer production, explicitly DIY aesthetic. Fastbacks, Flop, Alcohol Funnycar. The underground punk end of the Seattle indie ecosystem. Not Sub Pop (commercial aspirations), not A&M/DGC (major), but the punk underground.
theory_nodes.description: Empty Records as the underground punk wing of Seattle indie: the smallest, most DIY label in the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: DAVE DEDERER TRAJECTORY. Dederer in Alcohol Funnycar (Empty Records, underground) -> co-founds PUSA with Chris Ballew (1993) -> PUSA debut on Maverick (1995) = 1M+ copies. Most dramatic underground-to-mainstream trajectory in the engine. Punk velocity developed at Empty carried to PUSA's economy of arrangement.
theory_nodes.description: Empty Records punk guitarist to Maverick Records 1M+ commercial act: the Dederer before-and-after.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 FROM VELOCITY. Four consecutive degrees (317-320) at E=7 in the post-summit cooldown, each from a different source: grief (Temple, 317), accessibility (Candlebox, 318), precision (Hammerbox, 319), velocity (Alcohol Funnycar, 320). The cooldown documents the full range of what E=7 means in Seattle grunge. The velocity mode: fast tempos, short songs, momentum over weight.
theory_nodes.description: E=7 from punk speed: the fourth mode. Momentum over weight, forward push over downward press.

theory_nodes.concept: PUNK END OF THE GRUNGE SPECTRUM. The engine has documented grunge from metal end (Melvins E=8, TAD E=8) through commercial center (Soundgarden, Pearl Jam, AIC) through Sub Pop punk (Mudhoney) to underground punk-grunge (Alcohol Funnycar). Degree 320 reaches the furthest punk end of the spectrum. The grunge-punk continuum is complete.
theory_nodes.description: Alcohol Funnycar as the engine's punk-end representative: the furthest from metal on the grunge spectrum.

cross_references.relationship: Jason Finn: Love Battery (221) + PUSA formation (1993). Dave Dederer: Alcohol Funnycar (320) + PUSA formation (1993). Two different pre-PUSA bands, same future destination. The PUSA connection runs through both degree 221 and degree 320.

Metal end of the grunge spectrum (E=8) vs. punk end (E=7). Degree 308 to degree 320 spans the full grunge spectrum from sludge metal to underground punk. The spectrum is now documented at both extremes.
E=7 fourth consecutive. Four modes: grief/accessibility/precision/velocity. The post-summit cooldown at E=7 is fully explored across four distinct mechanisms.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. FIRST CORVIDAE pairs with FIRST APPEARANCE. Both are firsts at degree 320. Raven: maximum vocabulary display, 30+ distinct call types, complex intelligence. Alcohol Funnycar: maximum punk economy, fast and direct. Pairing theme: COMPLEXITY VS. SIMPLICITY BOTH AT E=7 -- Raven's 30 vocalizations and AFC's 2-minute songs both calibrate to E=7 through different routes.
